Package org.eventb.core.pm

Interface IProofComponent

All Known Implementing Classes:
ProofComponent
public interface IProofComponent
Common protocol for accessing the proofs of an event-B component. This interface provides a unique facade for manipulating proofs in a consistent manner.

Proof components are created by calling the getProofComponent method of the proof manager. From a proof component, one can create a new proof attempt for a proof obligation.

The proof related data of an event-B component are stored in three different files:

  • the PO file contains the lemmas of the proof obligations generated for the component;
  • the PR file contains the proofs created by the user or the auto-prover;
  • the PS file contains the statuses of the proof obligations, i.e., whether the proofs stored in the PR file do discharge the lemmas contained in the PO file.

This class provides utility method that allow to manipulate both the proof file and the proof status file of a proof component together.

All interactions with the Rodin database are done with a scheduling rule locking these three Rodin files.

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IProofManager.getProofComponent(IEventBRoot)
  • IProofAttempt

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    IProofAttempt
    createProofAttempt(String poName, String owner, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
    Creates a new proof attempt for the given proof obligation and owner.
    FormulaFactory
    getFormulaFactory()
    Deprecated.
    use getSafeFormulaFactory() and handle the exception
    IPORoot
    getPORoot()
    Returns the Rodin root associated with this proof component and containing its proof obligations.
    IProofAttempt
    getProofAttempt(String poName, String owner)
    Returns the proof attempt that has been created for the given proof obligation and owner, and not disposed so far.
    IProofAttempt[]
    getProofAttempts()
    Returns an array of all proof attempts that have been created for this component and not disposed so far.
    IProofAttempt[]
    getProofAttempts(String poName)
    Returns an array of all proof attempts that have been created for the given proof obligation and not disposed so far.
    IProofSkeleton
    getProofSkeleton(String poName, FormulaFactory factory, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
    Returns the proof corresponding to the given proof obligation as a proof skeleton.
    IPRRoot
    getPRRoot()
    Returns the Rodin root associated with this proof component and containing its proofs.
    IPSRoot
    getPSRoot()
    Returns the Rodin root associated with this proof component and containing its proof statuses.
    FormulaFactory
    getSafeFormulaFactory()
    Returns the formula factory which is used for constructing new proof attempts.
    org.eclipse.core.runtime.jobs.ISchedulingRule
    getSchedulingRule()
    Returns the scheduling rule used by this component for interacting with the Rodin database.
    IPSStatus
    getStatus(String poName)
    Returns a handle to the proof status for the proof obligation with the given name.
    boolean
    hasUnsavedChanges()
    Tells whether the proof file or the proof status file of this component has unsaved changes.
    void
    makeConsistent(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
    Makes the proof file and the proof status file of this component consistent with their underlying resources.
    void
    save(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor, boolean force)
    Save both the proof and proof status file of this proof component.

  • Method Details

    • createProofAttempt

      IProofAttempt createProofAttempt(String poName, String owner, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Creates a new proof attempt for the given proof obligation and owner.

      This is a long running operation which is scheduled using the rule of this proof component.

      Parameters:
      poName - name of the proof obligation to load for the proof attempt
      owner - name of the owner of the proof attempt to create
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Returns:
      a new proof attempt
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if a proof attempt with the same characteristics has already been created and not disposed since
      RodinDBException - if there is some problem loading the corresponding proof obligation
      See Also:
      • getSchedulingRule()

    • getPORoot

      IPORoot getPORoot()
      Returns the Rodin root associated with this proof component and containing its proof obligations.
      Returns:
      the proof obligation root of this component.

    • getPRRoot

      IPRRoot getPRRoot()
      Returns the Rodin root associated with this proof component and containing its proofs.
      Returns:
      the proof root of this component.

    • getProofAttempts

      IProofAttempt[] getProofAttempts()
      Returns an array of all proof attempts that have been created for this component and not disposed so far.
      Returns:
      an array of all live proof attempts of this component

    • getProofAttempts

      IProofAttempt[] getProofAttempts(String poName)
      Returns an array of all proof attempts that have been created for the given proof obligation and not disposed so far. No attempt is made to check whether the given proof obligation actually exists.
      Parameters:
      poName - name of a proof obligation of this component
      Returns:
      an array of all live proof attempts for the given proof obligation

    • getProofAttempt

      IProofAttempt getProofAttempt(String poName, String owner)
      Returns the proof attempt that has been created for the given proof obligation and owner, and not disposed so far. No attempt is made to check whether the given proof obligation actually exists.
      Parameters:
      poName - name of a proof obligation of this component
      owner - name of the owner of the proof attempt to retrieve
      Returns:
      the live proof attempt for the given proof obligation and owner, or null if no such proof attempt exists
      Since:
      2.0

    • getProofSkeleton

      IProofSkeleton getProofSkeleton(String poName, FormulaFactory factory, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Returns the proof corresponding to the given proof obligation as a proof skeleton. The result is read from the proof file of this component.

      This is a long running operation which is scheduled using the rule of this proof component.

      Parameters:
      poName - name of a proof obligation
      factory - the formula factory to use for building the proof skeleton
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Returns:
      the proof skeleton for the given proof obligation
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there is some problem loading the corresponding proof
      See Also:
      • getSchedulingRule()

    • getFormulaFactory

      @Deprecated FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Deprecated.
      use getSafeFormulaFactory() and handle the exception
      Returns the formula factory which is used for constructing new proof attempts. It is also the factory to use for parsing the proof obligations of this component.
      Returns:
      the formula factory of the proof obligations
      Since:
      3.0

    • getSafeFormulaFactory

      FormulaFactory getSafeFormulaFactory() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the formula factory which is used for constructing new proof attempts. It is also the factory to use for parsing the proof obligations of this component.
      Returns:
      the formula factory of the proof obligations
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if the formula extension provider failed
      Since:
      3.7

    • getPSRoot

      IPSRoot getPSRoot()
      Returns the Rodin root associated with this proof component and containing its proof statuses.
      Returns:
      the proof status root of this component.

    • getSchedulingRule

      org.eclipse.core.runtime.jobs.ISchedulingRule getSchedulingRule()
      Returns the scheduling rule used by this component for interacting with the Rodin database.
      Returns:
      the scheduling rule for this component

    • getStatus

      IPSStatus getStatus(String poName)
      Returns a handle to the proof status for the proof obligation with the given name.

      This is a handle-only method. The status element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      poName - name of a proof obligation
      Returns:
      the proof status for the given proof obligation

    • hasUnsavedChanges

      boolean hasUnsavedChanges() throws RodinDBException
      Tells whether the proof file or the proof status file of this component has unsaved changes.
      Returns:
      true if the proof file or the proof status file of this component has unsaved changes.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if one of the proof file or the proof status file does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing their corresponding resources.
      See Also:
      • IOpenable.hasUnsavedChanges()

    • makeConsistent

      void makeConsistent(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Makes the proof file and the proof status file of this component consistent with their underlying resources.
      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if one of the proof file or proof status file is unable to access the contents of its underlying resource. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      See Also:
      • IOpenable.makeConsistent(IProgressMonitor)

    • save

      void save(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor, boolean force) throws RodinDBException
      Save both the proof and proof status file of this proof component. The parameters of this method are exactly the same as those of the IOpenable.save(IProgressMonitor, boolean) method.
      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      force - it controls how this method deals with cases where the workbench is not completely in sync with the local file system
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs accessing the contents of the underlying resource of the proof or proof status file. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • This Rodin element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      See Also:
      • IOpenable.save(IProgressMonitor, boolean)
